# oasdiff --err-ignore file: breaking API changes intentionally accepted for the
# dev -> main promotion (PR #157 and future major promotions that carry these).
#
# Matching: oasdiff lower-cases each finding to a single line and ignores it when
# a line below is a CONTIGUOUS SUBSTRING of that text. Keep every entry narrow
# (operation + path + exact message) so UNRELATED / FUTURE breaking changes still
# fail the gate. Lines starting with `#` and blank lines are inert (they never
# match a finding). Verified against oasdiff v1.18.1.
#
# Each accepted change below is also pinned by a test, so it cannot silently
# regress:

# 1. GET /health is an operational liveness probe, intentionally excluded from the
#    public OpenAPI document (it carries no versioned API contract).
api get /health api path removed without deprecation

# 2. POST /api/v1/auth/mfa/challenge was removed in the #219 security hardening; the
#    route is no longer publicly exposed (auth-mfa.integration.test.ts pins this).
api post /api/v1/auth/mfa/challenge api path removed without deprecation

# 3. POST /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods tightened `method_type` to a fixed enum so the
#    DTO rejects manual provider-identity binding (auth-method.service.unit.test.ts).
api post /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods request property `method_type` was restricted to a list of enum values

# 4. POST /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/:id/notification-policies tightened `channel` to the
#    EMAIL/SMS/PUSH/IN_APP enum so an unknown channel is a 422 at the edge, not a 500 from the
#    chk_org_notif_channel database check (organization-notification-policy.validator.unit.test.ts).
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/notification-policies request property `channel` was restricted to a list of enum values

# 5. PUT /api/v1/users/me/notification-preferences tightened the per-preference `channel` to the
#    same enum so an unknown channel is a 422, not a 500 from the chk_user_notif_prefs_channel
#    database check (user-notification-preferences.validator.unit.test.ts).
api put /api/v1/users/me/notification-preferences request property `preferences/items/channel` was restricted to a list of enum values

# 6. Array maxItems bounds added as a security hardening measure (audit finding #1-#4).
#    Clients sending >50 webhook event types, >200 notification preferences,
#    >200 permission codes, or >10 preferred locales receive a 422 instead of
#    an oversized DB write. Legitimate payloads are unaffected.
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/webhooks request property `events` max items set to
api patch /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/webhooks/{webhookid} request property `events` max items set to
api put /api/v1/users/me/notification-preferences request property `preferences` max items set to
api put /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/roles/{roleid}/permissions request property `permission_codes` max items set to
api patch /api/v1/users/me/settings request property `preferred_locales` max items set to

# 7. POST /api/v1/auth/webauthn/authenticate/options tightened `email` from optional to
#    required (sec-A finding #24). The DTO was inconsistent with the service's
#    anti-enumeration decoy path — the service threw `errors:invalidEmailOrPassword`
#    when `email` was absent (a present-vs-absent oracle) while returning a
#    structurally-identical decoy challenge for unknown emails. Making `email`
#    required at the DTO matches the production behaviour (webauthn.validator.unit.test.ts
#    + webauthn.service.authenticate-options.unit.test.ts pin this).
api post /api/v1/auth/webauthn/authenticate/options the request property `email` became required

# 8. POST /api/v1/notify/organizations/{id}/webhooks and PATCH
#    .../webhooks/{webhookId} now require `secret` to be ≥16 chars (sec-UP finding
#    #8). The prior `.max(255).optional()` accepted empty strings, which on the
#    worker path round-tripped through `decryptFieldSecret('')` and emitted a
#    deterministic `createHmac('sha256', '')` value — customers verifying the
#    signature would accept any forged request signed with the same empty key.
#    Pinned by webhook.dto.unit.test.ts + webhook.service.unit.test.ts.
api post /api/v1/notify/organizations/{id}/webhooks the `secret` request property's minlength was increased from `0` to `16`
api patch /api/v1/notify/organizations/{id}/webhooks/{webhookid} the `secret` request property's minlength was increased from `0` to `16`

# 9. POST /api/v1/uploads tightened `fileSize` with a `.max()` defense-in-depth
#    cap (sec-r4-I4). The validator has always rejected any value above the
#    relevant per-purpose maxSize from UPLOAD_PURPOSE_CONFIG (2 MB / 5 MB / 10 MB),
#    so the DTO change makes the OpenAPI contract truthful — clients that were
#    sending oversized values were already getting 422s. Pinned by
#    upload.validator.mime-and-size.unit.test.ts.
api post /api/v1/uploads the `filesize` request property's max was decreased to `10485760.00`

# 10. DELETE /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods/{id} renamed path param from bigserial `{id}` to
#     `{publicId}` (21-char opaque identifier) as part of Round 3 security hardening sec-new-B4.
#     Exposing the bigserial id allows row-count enumeration. Callers must use the public-id path.
#     Pinned by auth.test.ts which asserts 400 for numeric (old) params.
api delete /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods/{id} removed the success response with the status `204`

# 11. POST /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods narrowed `method_type` from the full auth-method enum to
#     MAGIC_LINK only (route-#3 security hardening). create() inserts a credential-less row, so
#     PASSWORD (needs a hash) / MFA_* (need an encrypted secret) / OAUTH (verified-callback only)
#     could only ever be inserted here as NON-FUNCTIONAL phantom rows that the last-login-capable
#     credential guard miscounted — letting a user delete their only real method and lock
#     themselves out. Only MAGIC_LINK is functional via this endpoint. Pinned by
#     auth.validator.unit.test.ts (rejects PASSWORD/OAUTH/MFA_*).
api post /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods removed the enum value `password` of the request property `method_type`
api post /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods removed the enum value `oauth` of the request property `method_type`
api post /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods removed the enum value `mfa_totp` of the request property `method_type`
api post /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods removed the enum value `mfa_sms` of the request property `method_type`
api post /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods removed the enum value `mfa_email` of the request property `method_type`

# 12. Spec-truth correction (PR #575): four operations documented a spurious `200`
#     success that the API never returned — POST /uploads and POST .../api-keys/
#     {apiKeyId}/rotate have always replied 201, DELETE /uploads/{publicId} and
#     DELETE /auth/me/auth-methods/{publicId} reply 204 (the latter's response-map
#     entry was keyed to the retired `{id}` param, so the generator fell back to a
#     generic 200). The success statuses now come from the runtime-verified
#     success-status registry (route-success-statuses.json); pinned by
#     route-response-map-statuses.unit.test.ts and the observed-status coverage gate.
api delete /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods/{publicid} removed the success response with the status `200`
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/api-keys/{apikeyid}/rotate removed the success response with the status `200`
api post /api/v1/uploads removed the success response with the status `200`
api delete /api/v1/uploads/{publicid} removed the success response with the status `200`
